heading: Sale of naturalization or citizenship papers
label: 1427 Sale of naturalization or citizenship papers
text: Whoever unlawfully sells or disposes of a declaration of intention to become a citizen, certificate of naturalization, certificate of citizenship or copies or duplicates or other documentary evidence of naturalization or citizenship, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 25 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate an act of international terrorism (as defined in )), 20 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate a drug trafficking crime (as defined in )), 10 years (in the case of the first or second such offense, if the offense was not committed to facilitate such an act of international terrorism or a drug trafficking crime), or 15 years (in the case of any other offense), or both.section 2331 of this titlesection 929(a) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on subsections (a) par. (13), (d) of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Aliens and Nationality (, (d), , 1167).section 746 of title 8Oct. 14, 1940, ch. 876, § 346(a)(13)54 Stat. 1165Minor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments2002— substituted “to facilitate” for “to facility”.Pub. L. 107–2731996— substituted “imprisoned not more than 25 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate an act of international terrorism (as defined in )), 20 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate a drug trafficking crime (as defined in )), 10 years (in the case of the first or second such offense, if the offense was not committed to facility such an act of international terrorism or a drug trafficking crime), or 15 years (in the case of any other offense)” for “imprisoned not more than five years”.Pub. L. 104–208section 2331 of this titlesection 929(a) of this title1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–322

Effective Date of 1996 AmendmentAmendment by applicable with respect to offenses occurring on or after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 104–208section 211(c) of Pub. L. 104–208section 1028 of this titleSept. 30, 1996
